steps.instruction: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is 70°.
steps.work: The 8 angles formed come in specific pairs with known relationships.
steps.explanation: A transversal creates 8 angles — 4 at each intersection point.

steps.instruction: Corresponding, alternate interior, and alternate exterior angles are EQUAL.
steps.work: Corresponding angle = 70°. Alternate interior angle = 70°. Alternate exterior angle = 70°.
steps.explanation: These pairs are on opposite sides or matching positions and are always equal with parallel lines.

steps.instruction: Co-interior (same-side interior) angles are supplementary (sum to 180°).
steps.explanation: Same-side interior angles always add to 180° when lines are parallel.

title: Solving for x with Parallel Lines

steps.instruction: Two parallel lines cut by a transversal form alternate interior angles: (2x + 10)° and (3x − 20)°.
steps.explanation: First identify which angle pair you have, then use the correct relationship.
Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel.
